points.text: Practice good personal hygiene, including regular hand washing with soap and water.
Wear a face mask in public and when in close contact with others.
Maintain physical or social distancing to reduce transmission risk.
Keep indoor spaces well-ventilated to minimize airborne transmission.
Avoid sharing personal household items to prevent virus spread.
Stay home if you are feeling unwell or believe you may be infected.
Cover coughs and sneezes with a tissue or your elbow.
Stay up to date on COVID-19 vaccinations to reduce infection risk.
Practice physical distancing from individuals showing respiratory symptoms.
Quarantine for a minimum of 10 days if exposed to COVID-19.
Stay up to date on your COVID-19 vaccine to prevent serious illness.
Ensure good indoor airflow to help prevent the spread of the virus.
Practice physical distancing to reduce the risk of transmission.
Wear a mask in appropriate settings to protect yourself and others.
Maintain good hygiene by washing hands frequently with soap and water.
Avoid close contact with anyone who is sick or has symptoms.
Test for COVID-19 if you have symptoms or after exposure to the virus.
Cover your coughs and sneezes to prevent spreading the virus.
Avoid crowded places and practice physical distancing, keeping at least 1 metre distance from others.
Wear a mask in public places where community transmission is present and physical distancing is not possible.
Wash your hands frequently with soap and water or an alcohol-based hand rub.
Keep indoor spaces well ventilated to reduce the risk of virus transmission.
Seek medical care early if you or your child show symptoms of COVID-19.
Stay informed about the latest COVID-19 guidance from local authorities.
Get vaccinated to help reduce the spread of the virus and its mutations.
Regularly clean and disinfect surfaces that are frequently touched.
